mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) is an always-on coincident demand signal that positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns because expanding consumer spending flows directly into corporate revenues.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market healing, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window
failure_condition: Positive 20-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when the UNRATE trigger fires over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous disinflation in CPIAUCSL and payroll expansion in PAYEMS represent a Goldilocks environment where rising real wages support demand without provoking monetary tightening, jointly driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change negative while PAYEMS 3-month change positive
failure_condition: The joint signal of CPIAUCSL and PAYEMS produces a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or either variable alone explains the return.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) predicts positive quarterly S&P 500 returns specifically when the economy is recovering from a deep output gap, as proxied by an elevated unemployment rate, because supply recovery is the binding constraint on profits.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) crossing above 600 thousand units signals a trough in residential construction, a leading cyclical sector whose recovery drives broad equity risk appetite and positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered weeks when HOUST is above 600 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero, an accelerating CPIAUCSL reading triggers a reflation trade that compresses real rates and boosts nominal corporate earnings expectations, producing sharply positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL signal generates a rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window within ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is a coincident measure of aggregate demand that persistently predicts positive S&P 500 quarterly returns because output expansion maps directly into sales and earnings growth across the index.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exhibits a rank IC below 0.02 or a statistically insignificant positive correlation with 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5 percent reflects severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and squeezes corporate margins through weak pricing power, triggering negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 9.5 percent.
